document: Electronic Identification and Trust Services (eIDAS) — UK retained version (United Kingdom - Row 8)

sections.heading: In Article 2—
sections.title: Electronic seal security level for cross-border services
sections.summary: Public sector bodies cannot require higher security levels for electronic seals when services are used outside the UK. This provision ensures that electronic seals used for cross-border services maintain a consistent security standard based on qualified electronic seals.

requirements.text: Public sector body may not require the seal to be at a higher security level than that of a qualified electronic seal for use from outside the United Kingdom

sections.source_quote: 3. If a public sector body requires an electronic seal to use an online service offered by or on behalf of that body, the body may not, for the use of that service from a place outside the United Kingdom, require the seal to be at a higher security level than that of a qualified electronic seal.

sections.heading: In Article 13—
sections.title: Amendments to Article 13 for UK and EU trust service providers
sections.summary: This section amends Article 13 by inserting 'established in the United Kingdom or in the EU' after 'trust service providers' in paragraph 1 and substituting 'general principles of liability in tort or delict' for 'national rules on liability' in paragraph 3.

requirements.text: In paragraph 1, in the first subparagraph, after 'trust service providers' insert 'established in the United Kingdom or in the EU'
In paragraph 3, for 'national rules on liability' substitute 'general principles of liability in tort or delict'

sections.source_quote: 8. In Article 13— (a) in paragraph 1, in the first subparagraph, after 'trust service providers' insert 'established in the United Kingdom or in the EU'; (b) in paragraph 3, for 'national rules on liability' substitute 'general principles of liability in tort or delict'.

sections.heading: Omit Article 15.
sections.title: Omission of Article 15
sections.summary: This section instructs the omission of Article 15 from the regulations. It notes that S.I. 2015/102 was amended by S.I. 2016/275 and 2016/696, but none of these amending instruments are relevant to the current context.

requirements.text: Omit Article 15

sections.source_quote: 10. Omit Article 15.

sections.heading: Omit Article 16.
sections.title: Amendments to Article 17 for UK supervisory body
sections.summary: This section amends Article 17 by inserting '(as assigned to the Information Commissioner by regulation 3 of the Electronic Identification and Trust Services for Electronic Transactions Regulations 2016 (S.I. 2016/696))' after 'supervisory body' in paragraph 3, substituting 'United Kingdom' for 'territory of the designating Member State' in points (a) and (b), and omitting points (a), (c), and (d) from paragraph 4. It also replaces paragraph 5 with a new provision allowing the Secretary of State to direct the supervisory body to establish, maintain, and update a trust infrastructure.

requirements.text: In paragraph 3— (a) in the words before point (a), after 'supervisory body' insert '(as assigned to the Information Commissioner by regulation 3 of the Electronic Identification and Trust Services for Electronic Transactions Regulations 2016 (S.I. 2016/696))'
In points (a) and (b), for 'territory of the designating Member State' substitute 'United Kingdom'
In paragraph 4— (a) omit point (a); (b) in point (c), omit 'other supervisory bodies and'; (c) omit point (d); (d) in point (h), omit 'national'

For paragraph 5 substitute '1. The Secretary of State may give directions to the supervisory body requiring it to establish, maintain and update a trust infrastructure in accordance with the directions.'

sections.source_quote: 11. Omit Article 16. 12.—(1) Article 17 is amended as follows. (2) Omit paragraphs 1 and 2. (3) In paragraph 3— (a) in the words before point (a), after 'supervisory body' insert '(as assigned to the Information Commissioner by regulation 3 of the Electronic Identification and Trust Services for Electronic Transactions Regulations 2016 (S.I. 2016/696))'; (b) in points (a) and (b), for 'territory of the designating Member State' substitute 'United Kingdom'. (4) In paragraph 4— (a) omit point (a); (b) in point (c), omit 'other supervisory bodies and'; (c) omit point (d); (d) in point (h), omit 'national'. (5) For paragraph 5 substitute— '1. The Secretary of State may give directions to the supervisory body requiring it to establish, maintain and update a trust infrastructure in accordance with the directions.' (6) Omit paragraphs 6 to 8.

sections.heading: The supervisory body may give information and assistance to, and otherwise co-
sections.title: Qualification equivalence for trust services
sections.summary: This section defines when a trust service is considered qualified under UK law if it is not qualified under the EU Regulation. It states that a service is treated as qualified if it meets equivalent EU law requirements or if applying assumptions in paragraph 2 results in qualification under either regulation. This creates a mechanism for cross-border recognition of trust services.
sections.dtia_summary: This section establishes equivalence mechanisms for trust services, allowing UK-qualified services to be recognized internationally without requiring separate compliance with EU regulations.

requirements.text: Anything not qualified under this Regulation is treated as qualified if it meets equivalent EU law
Or if applying assumptions in paragraph 2 results in qualification under this Regulation or equivalent EU law

sections.source_quote: 1. For the purposes of Articles 25(2), 27, 35(2), 37, 41(2) and 43(2) (and any implementing measures having effect for the purposes of those provisions), anything which is not qualified under this Regulation is to be treated as qualified if: (a) it is qualified under the equivalent EU law, or (b) the application of any one or more of the assumptions in paragraph 2 would result in its being qualified under either this Regulation or the equivalent EU law.

sections.heading: Nothing in paragraph 1 authorises the processing of personal data other than in
sections.title: Public sector electronic seals for online services
sections.summary: Public sector bodies must accept advanced electronic seals based on qualified certificates for online services. This applies to electronic seals that comply with the Implementing Decision. The requirement ensures interoperability and trust in digital signatures across public services.

requirements.text: Public sector body must recognise any advanced electronic seal based on a qualified certificate for electronic seal
Public sector body must recognise any qualified electronic seal

sections.source_quote: 2. If a public sector body requires an advanced electronic seal based on a qualified certificate for electronic seal to use an online service offered by or on behalf of that body, the body must recognise any advanced electronic seal based on a qualified certificate for electronic seal, or any qualified electronic seal, that complies with the Implementing Decision.

sections.heading: In Article 20—
sections.title: Amendment of Data Protection Act 2018 for eIDAS trust services
sections.summary: This section states that Regulation 3 of the Data Protection Act 2018 was amended by section 211(1)(b) of the Data Protection Act 2018 and paragraphs 403 and 405 of Schedule 19 to the Data Protection Act 2018. The amendment modifies the wording of Regulation 3 by substituting 'lists' with 'list' and omitting paragraph 4. The section does not impose new obligations but describes the amendment process for the Data Protection Act 2018.

requirements.text: Regulation 3 was amended by section 211(1)(b) of, and paragraphs 403 and 405 of Schedule 19 to, the Data Protection Act 2018 (c. 12)

sections.source_quote: 15. In Article 20— (4) Regulation 3 was amended by section 211(1)(b) of, and paragraphs 403 and 405 of Schedule 19 to, the Data Protection Act 2018 (c. 12). (5) 2018 c. 12.

sections.heading: In Article 21—
sections.title: Trust service providers must be UK-established
sections.summary: This section requires that in Article 21, paragraph 1 of the regulation must be amended to specify that trust service providers must be established in the United Kingdom. It also mandates substituting 'lists' with 'list' in paragraphs 2 and 3, and omitting paragraph 4 of the regulation. The section outlines specific textual modifications to the regulation without imposing new obligations.

requirements.text: In paragraph 1, after 'providers' insert 'established in the United Kingdom'
In paragraph 2, in the second subparagraph, for 'lists' substitute 'list'
In paragraph 3, for 'lists' substitute 'list'
Omit paragraph 4

sections.source_quote: 16. In Article 21— (a) in paragraph 1, after 'providers' insert 'established in the United Kingdom'; (b) in paragraph 2, in the second subparagraph, for 'lists' substitute 'list'; (c) in paragraph 3, for 'lists' substitute 'list'; (d) omit paragraph 4.

sections.title: Qualification equivalence for trust services

This creates a mechanism for cross-border recognition of trust services.

Or if applying assumptions in paragraph 2 results in qualification under this Regulation or equivalent EU law

For the purposes of Articles 25(2), 27, 35(2), 37, 41(2) and 43(2) (and any implementing measures having effect for the purposes of those provisions), anything which is not qualified under this Regulation is to be treated as qualified if: (a) it is qualified under the equivalent EU law, or (b) the application of any one or more of the assumptions in paragraph 2 would result in its being qualified under either this Regulation or the equivalent EU law.

sections.heading: The arrangements must provide for the maintenance and publication of the trusted
sections.title: Public sector electronic seals for online services
sections.summary: Public sector bodies must accept advanced electronic seals based on qualified certificates for online services. This applies to electronic seals that comply with the Implementing Decision. The requirement ensures interoperability and trust in digital signatures across public services.

requirements.text: Public sector body must recognise any advanced electronic seal based on a qualified certificate for electronic seal
Public sector body must recognise any qualified electronic seal

sections.source_quote: 2. If a public sector body requires an advanced electronic seal based on a qualified certificate for electronic seal to use an online service offered by or on behalf of that body, the body must recognise any advanced electronic seal based on a qualified certificate for electronic seal, or any qualified electronic seal, that complies with the Implementing Decision.

sections.heading: The arrangements may provide for the trusted list to include information relating to
sections.title: Trusted list may include non-qualified UK trust service providers
sections.summary: The arrangements may include information about trust service providers established in the United Kingdom that do not have qualified status, along with the trust services they provide. If included, the list must clearly indicate which providers and services are not qualified. This allows for a distinction between qualified and non-qualified providers.

requirements.text: The arrangements may provide for the trusted list to include information relating to trust service providers established in the United Kingdom that do not have qualified status
The arrangements must also provide for the list to indicate clearly which providers and services are not qualified

sections.source_quote: 4. The arrangements may provide for the trusted list to include information relating to trust service providers established in the United Kingdom that do not have qualified status, and the trust services provided by them. Where the arrangements do so, they must also provide for the list to indicate clearly which providers and services are not qualified.

sections.heading: The arrangements must provide for the publication, in a form that is electronically
sections.title: Publication of trusted list details
sections.summary: The arrangements must publish specific information about the trusted list, including details of the body responsible for the list, where the list is published, the certificates used to sign or seal the list, and any changes to the list. This information must be published in an electronically signed or sealed format suitable for automated processing.

requirements.text: The arrangements must provide for the publication, in a form that is electronically signed or sealed and suitable for automated processing, of information on the body referred to in paragraph 3
The arrangements must provide for the publication, in a form that is electronically signed or sealed and suitable for automated processing, of details of where the trusted list is published
The arrangements must provide for the publication, in a form that is electronically signed or sealed and suitable for automated processing, of the certificates used to sign or seal the list
The arrangements must provide for the publication, in a form that is electronically signed or sealed and suitable for automated processing, of any changes thereto

sections.source_quote: 5. The arrangements must provide for the publication, in a form that is electronically signed or sealed and suitable for automated processing, of: (a) information on the body referred to in paragraph 3, and (b) details of where the trusted list is published, the certificates used to sign or seal the list, and any changes thereto.

sections.heading: The trusted list maintained under this Article is initially to consist of the information
sections.title: Initial trusted list composition for eIDAS
sections.summary: This section specifies that the initial trusted list for electronic identification services must include the information from the pre-exit list under Article 22 of the EU Regulation. The list is defined as the information existing immediately before the UK's exit day from the EU. This establishes a continuity mechanism for trusted services during the transition period.
sections.dtia_summary: The initial trusted list composition ensures continuity of trusted electronic identification services across borders during the UK's exit from the EU. This affects how cross-border data transfers for electronic signatures are handled.

requirements.text: The trusted list initially consists of the information from the pre-exit list under Article 22

sections.source_quote: 6. The trusted list maintained under this Article is initially to consist of the information that was in the list maintained immediately before exit day under Article 22 of this Regulation as it then had effect.

sections.heading: Omit Article 23.
sections.title: Amendment of Article 24 for eIDAS trust services
sections.summary: This section amends Article 24 to adjust the criteria for qualified trust services. It removes references to national law compliance and specifies that assurance levels must align with equivalent EU law for electronic identification schemes. The changes ensure that trust services meet standardized international requirements without relying on national legal frameworks.
sections.dtia_summary: The amendment clarifies that trust services must meet equivalent EU assurance levels for electronic identification, ensuring consistent international standards for cross-border data transfers involving electronic signatures.

requirements.text: Omit references to national law compliance in assurance level requirements
Assurance levels must be 'substantial' or 'high' under equivalent EU law
Remove national-level recognition requirements for trust services

sections.source_quote: 18. Omit Article 23. 19.—(1) Article 24 is amended as follows. (2) In paragraph 1— (a) in the first subparagraph, omit "and in accordance with national law"; (b) in the second subparagraph— (i) in the words before point (a), omit "in accordance with national law"; (ii) in point (b), for the words from "set out" to "‘high'" substitute "for the assurance levels ‘substantial' or ‘high' under the equivalent EU law so far as relating to electronic identification schemes (or would meet those requirements if they were not predicated on the doing of anything in, or by, a member State)"; (iii) in point (d), omit "recognised at a national level".

sections.heading: In Article 25, omit paragraph 3.
sections.title: Amendment of Article 27 for public sector trust services
sections.summary: This section amends Article 27 to require public sector bodies to recognize advanced electronic signatures that comply with the Implementing Decision, regardless of whether they are based on qualified certificates. This ensures that public sector entities can accept electronic signatures from a wider range of providers without requiring specific certificate validation.
sections.dtia_summary: This amendment ensures public sector bodies accept advanced electronic signatures without certificate validation, facilitating cross-border data transfers for public services.

requirements.text: Public sector bodies must recognize advanced electronic signatures that comply with the Implementing Decision

sections.source_quote: 21. In Article 25, omit paragraph 3. 22.—(1) Article 27 is amended as follows. (2) For paragraphs 1 to 3 substitute— "1. sections.heading: In Article 29, omit paragraph 2.
sections.title: Supervisory body list maintenance for electronic signatures
sections.summary: The supervisory body must maintain and publish a list of electronic signature creation devices whose certification has been notified to it. This applies to the supervisory body designated under Article 30. The requirement ensures transparency and accountability in the electronic signature ecosystem.

requirements.text: The supervisory body must maintain and publish a list of electronic signature creation devices the certification of which is notified to it under paragraph 1

sections.source_quote: 2. The supervisory body must maintain and publish a list of electronic signature creation devices the certification of which is notified to it under paragraph 1.

sections.heading: In Article 31—
sections.title: Designated bodies must notify certification changes
sections.summary: Bodies designated under Article 30 must promptly notify the supervisory body of any certification of conformity they make or cancel for the purposes of Article 30. This applies to designated bodies providing electronic signature services. The requirement ensures timely updates to the supervisory body's records.

requirements.text: A body designated under Article 30(1) must notify the supervisory body as soon as reasonably practicable of any certification of conformity that it makes, or cancels, for the purposes of Article 30

sections.source_quote: 1. A body designated under Article 30(1) must notify the supervisory body as soon as reasonably practicable of any certification of conformity that it makes, or cancels, for the purposes of Article 30.

sections.heading: In Annex I, in point (b), omit ", the Member State in which that provider is established and".
sections.title: Annex I amendment for provider establishment
sections.summary: This section removes a reference to the Member State where a provider is established from Annex I, point (b). The change simplifies the definition of providers by excluding geographical location requirements.

requirements.text: Omit ', the Member State in which that provider is established and'

sections.source_quote: 42. In Annex I, in point (b), omit ', the Member State in which that provider is established and'.

sections.heading: Commission Decision of 16 October 2009 setting out measures facilitating the use of
sections.title: Revocation of 2009 electronic procedures decision
sections.summary: The Commission Decision of 16 October 2009, which facilitated electronic procedures through 'points of contact', is revoked. This decision was part of the internal market services framework under Directive 2006/123/EC.

requirements.text: Commission Decision of 16 October 2009 setting out measures facilitating the use of procedures by electronic means through the 'points of contact' under Directive 2006/123/EC is revoked

sections.title: Revocation of 2010 certification providers decision
sections.summary: The Commission Decision of 28 July 2010, which amended Decision 2009/767/EC regarding trusted lists of certification service providers, is revoked. This decision was part of the internal market services framework.

sections.title: Revocation of trusted certification service provider lists decision
sections.summary: This section revokes Commission Implementing Decision 2013/662/EU which established trusted lists of certification service providers supervised by Member States. The revocation is replaced by Commission Implementing Decision 2014/148/EU. The decision pertains to electronic identification and trust services within the EU framework.

sections.title: Revocation of electronic identification cooperation procedures
sections.summary: This section revokes Commission Implementing Decision establishing procedural arrangements for cooperation between Member States on electronic identification. sections.title: Revocation of EU trust mark specifications

sections.heading: Commission Implementing Decision (EU) 2015/1505 laying down technical specifications
sections.title: Revocation of EU technical specifications for trusted lists
sections.summary: This section revokes Commission Implementing Decision (EU) 2015/1505 which laid down technical specifications for trusted lists under the eIDAS Regulation. It also amends Commission Implementing Decision (EU) 2015/1506 to update specifications for advanced electronic signatures and seals, requiring trust service providers to be established in the UK or EU and public sector bodies to offer validation capabilities for automated processing.
sections.dtia_summary: This section is irrelevant to Data Transfer Impact Assessments as it deals with revocation of EU technical specifications for trusted lists and amendments to electronic signature standards, not data transfers.

requirements.text: Revokes Commission Implementing Decision (EU) 2015/1505 laying down technical specifications for trusted lists
Amends Commission Implementing Decision (EU) 2015/1506 to update specifications for advanced electronic signatures and seals
Trust service providers must be established in the UK or EU
Public sector bodies must offer signature validation capabilities for automated processing

sections.source_quote: Commission Implementing Decision (EU) 2015/1505 laying down technical specifications and formats relating to trusted lists pursuant to Article 22(5) of Regulation (EU) No 910/2014 is revoked.

sections.heading: Commission Implementing Decision (EU) 2015/1984 of 3 November 2015 defining the
sections.title: Revocation of EU notification procedures for trust services
sections.summary: This section revokes Commission Implementing Decision (EU) 2015/1984 which defined notification procedures for trust services under the eIDAS Regulation. It also amends Commission Implementing Decision (EU) 2016/650 to update security assessment standards for qualified signature and seal creation devices, removing references to specific devices and notification requirements.
sections.dtia_summary: This section is irrelevant to Data Transfer Impact Assessments as it deals with revocation of EU notification procedures for trust services, not data transfers.

requirements.text: Revokes Commission Implementing Decision (EU) 2015/1984 defining notification procedures for trust services
Amends Commission Implementing Decision (EU) 2016/650 to update security assessment standards for qualified signature and seal creation devices

sections.source_quote: Commission Implementing Decision (EU) 2015/1984 of 3 November 2015 defining the circumstances, formats and procedures of notification pursuant to Article 9(5) of Regulation (EU) No 910/2014 is revoked.

sections.heading: In the Electronic Identification and Trust Services for Electronic Transactions Regulations
sections.title: Amendment to eIDAS Regulations for UK trust services
sections.summary: This section amends the Electronic Identification and Trust Services for Electronic Transactions Regulations 2016 by removing regulation 6(2) which required review of eIDAS Regulation implementation in other Member States. It preserves the regulatory framework for UK trust services, ensuring interoperability with EU trust services while removing reciprocal arrangements no longer applicable post-Brexit.
sections.dtia_summary: This section is relevant for Data Transfer Impact Assessments as it preserves UK trust services framework and removes obsolete EU mutual recognition mechanisms, affecting how electronic signatures and seals are validated across borders.

requirements.text: Omits regulation 6(2) (review of eIDAS Regulation to have regard to implementation in other Member States)
Preserves regulatory framework for UK trust services
Ensures interoperability with EU trust services post-Brexit

sections.source_quote: In the Electronic Identification and Trust Services for Electronic Transactions Regulations 2016, omit regulation 6(2) (review of eIDAS Regulation to have regard to implementation in other Member States).
